frameworks.name: model-based vs model-free reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: dual-system RL arbitration between flexible planning and computationally cheap habits

frameworks.name: lifespan developmental trajectory of cognitive control
frameworks.one_line: model-based control emerges, peaks, and declines across the lifespan

frameworks.name: incentive-driven cognitive effort
frameworks.one_line: monetary incentives tested as a lever to recruit costly planning

core_question: Can monetary incentives boost model-based control in older adults whose planning is normatively declining?
key_assumptions: habits and plans correspond to model-free and model-based control
deploying model-based control is resource-costly and incentive-sensitive
manipulations: presence vs absence of monetary incentives for accurate choices
task familiarity (experienced online sample vs naive sample)
two experiments with older adult populations
key_findings: Experiment 1: incentives increased model-based control in an online older-adult sample
Experiment 2: a naive older-adult sample did not show an incentive-driven boost
outcome depended on prior task experience
interpretations: incentives can recruit model-based control in older adults but only with prior task exposure
age-related decline in planning is not strictly fixed and may be intervention-tractable
limitations_noted: discrepancy between samples leaves the generality of the incentive effect uncertain
